skills.name: Data Fundamentals
skills.description: Core concepts of data including collection, cleaning, exploration, and basic analysis techniques.
skills.notes: Covers data types, data sources, data quality, and lifecycle. Includes understanding how data is collected, cleaned, explored, and visualized before analysis.

skills.name: Excel for Data Analysis
skills.description: Using Excel to perform data analysis, reporting, and quick insights through formulas and visual tools.
skills.notes: Includes functions like VLOOKUP, IF, SUM, AVERAGE, pivot tables, and charts. Excel is often the first tool for analysts in real-world scenarios.

skills.name: SQL & Databases
skills.description: Querying and managing data stored in relational databases using Structured Query Language.
skills.notes: Learn SELECT queries, joins, aggregations, indexing, and database design. SQL is essential for retrieving and analyzing large datasets.

skills.name: Programming (Python/R)
skills.description: Using programming languages to manipulate, analyze, and automate data workflows.
skills.notes: Focus on Python (Pandas, NumPy) or R (dplyr). Learn data manipulation, scripting, and automation for scalable analysis.

skills.name: Data Collection
skills.description: Gathering data from multiple sources such as databases, APIs, files, and web scraping.
skills.notes: Includes working with CSV files, REST APIs, and scraping tools. Understanding how to collect reliable data is crucial for analysis.

skills.name: Data Cleaning & Preparation
skills.description: Preparing raw data by handling missing values, duplicates, and inconsistencies before analysis.
skills.notes: Includes data transformation, normalization, removing outliers, and using libraries like Pandas or dplyr to clean datasets.

skills.name: Data Visualization
skills.description: Creating charts and visual representations to communicate insights effectively.
skills.notes: Tools include Tableau, Power BI, Matplotlib, Seaborn, and ggplot2. Learn when to use bar charts, line graphs, scatter plots, and dashboards.

skills.name: Statistics & Probability
skills.description: Mathematical foundation for analyzing data and drawing meaningful conclusions.
skills.notes: Includes mean, median, variance, standard deviation, distributions, hypothesis testing, correlation, and regression.

skills.name: Machine Learning Basics
skills.description: Introduction to algorithms that allow systems to learn patterns and make predictions from data.
skills.notes: Covers supervised and unsupervised learning, regression, classification, clustering (K-means), and model evaluation techniques.

skills.name: Big Data Technologies
skills.description: Tools and frameworks for processing and analyzing large-scale datasets.
skills.notes: Includes Hadoop, Spark, distributed computing, parallel processing, and handling large datasets efficiently.
